skill.name: desmos-graphing
skill.description: Create accurate Desmos graph blocks for Obsidian notes, including functions, points, styles, restrictions, and viewport settings.
skill.summary: Create Desmos graph blocks for Obsidian math notes.
skill.author: BitYoungjae

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found markdown examples and Desmos syntax only. The apparent Ruby backticks are fenced code spans, and the weak crypto hits are words such as Desmos, dashed, and hex color examples. No prompt injection, command execution, network access, filesystem access, or malicious intent was found.

content.value_statement: Math notes often fail when graph syntax mixes settings, LaTeX, and restrictions. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code write correct desmos-graph blocks.
actual_capabilities: Builds desmos-graph code blocks with optional settings and equations.
Explains when to use mathjs syntax, LaTeX syntax, and plain restriction syntax.
Adds colors, dashed or dotted styles, point markers, labels, hidden equations, and noline tags.
Creates examples for functions, points, implicit equations, parametric curves, polar curves, and piecewise functions.
Troubleshoots common parser errors from pi, sqrt, pipes, unsupported colors, and malformed restrictions.
Suggests viewport bounds, graph dimensions, grid options, and axis labels for Obsidian notes.
limitations: It documents Obsidian desmos-graph syntax but does not install or run the plugin.
It cannot confirm rendered graph output without the user testing inside Obsidian.
Restrictions need numeric constants for pi and do not support LaTeX commands.
Named colors are limited to the supported list unless hex colors are used.

use_cases.title: Prepare Calculus Notes
use_cases.description: Create graph blocks for trigonometric functions, derivatives, integrals, and special points in Obsidian lessons.

use_cases.title: Document Mathematical Models
use_cases.description: Add styled function plots, inequalities, parametric curves, and labeled reference points to research notes.

use_cases.title: Debug Existing Graph Blocks
use_cases.description: Fix parser errors caused by pi syntax, sqrt syntax, unsupported colors, label delimiters, and restrictions.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Function Graph
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Obsidian desmos-graph block for y equals x squared and y equals sin x. Use clear colors and suitable bounds.

prompt_templates.title: Styled Points and Labels
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a desmos-graph block with a cosine curve, labeled points at 0, pi over 2, pi, and 3 pi over 2, and correct point syntax.

prompt_templates.title: Restriction Debugging
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this desmos-graph block and fix any pi, sqrt, color, label, or restriction syntax errors for Obsidian.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Curve Conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert an unsupported polar curve into a Desmos-compatible parametric curve and include viewport settings and styling.

output_examples.input: A teacher asks for a sine and cosine comparison graph.
output_examples.output: A ready-to-paste Obsidian graph block with radians, matching bounds, colored sine and cosine curves, and one dashed reference line.

output_examples.input: A student has a graph that fails when using pi in a point coordinate.
output_examples.output: A corrected explanation that points use LaTeX pi, settings use plain pi, and restrictions need numeric constants.

output_examples.input: A researcher wants a styled Bezier-style curve with control points.
output_examples.output: A graph block with a parametric curve, labeled control points, gray construction lines using hex colors, and stable viewport settings.

best_practices: Use mathjs syntax in settings, LaTeX syntax in equations and points, and plain math in restrictions.
Use hex colors for unsupported names such as gray, pink, brown, navy, and silver.
Test complex curves in small parts before combining styles, labels, restrictions, and hidden helper functions.
anti_patterns: Do not use LaTeX commands inside viewport settings or restrictions.
Do not use pipe characters inside equations or labels because pipes split graph segments.
Do not rely on unsupported color names when a precise hex color is available.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates and fixes Obsidian desmos-graph code blocks for mathematical graphs and annotations.

faq.question: Does it require the Desmos plugin for Obsidian?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill writes syntax for the Obsidian Desmos graphing plugin, but it does not install the plugin.

faq.question: Why does pi syntax change between sections?
faq.answer: Settings use mathjs syntax, while equations and points use Desmos LaTeX syntax.

faq.question: Can it handle parametric and polar curves?
faq.answer: Yes. It can write parametric curves and can suggest conversions for unsupported nonlinear polar equations.

faq.question: Can labels include vertical bars?
faq.answer: No. The pipe character separates graph segments, so labels should use another symbol for norms or absolute values.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed files contain markdown documentation and examples only, with no executable scripts or network behavior.
